title: Job Finding Assistance - 211info
text: Job Finding Assistance - 211info - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Employment > Job Finding Assistance 64 Matching Service Providers Refine Results - Select - No Filter Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues Adults With Disabilities/Health Conditions African American Community Homelessness Issues Seasonal Workers Substance Use Disorder Issues Veterans 4TH DIMENSION RECOVERY CENTER CLACKAMAS CENTER ADDICTION RECOVERY PEER MENTORING SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers peer mentoring services for substance use disorders. Mentors help clients to find housing, employment, applying for benefits such as TANF and OHP, guidance about legal aid programs, and transportation to recovery-oriented meetings and events. Also offers 12-step meetings and recovery-related activities and events, including dances, barbecues, poetry nights, and movie nights. 11690 SE 82nd Avenue Happy Valley, OR 97086 Eligibility: Individuals age 14-35 with substance use issues. Clients can be currently in treatment, just leaving treatment, or at any other stage of their recovery process. Hours: 7 days per week 2pm-10pm Email (971) 703-46

title: Summer and Heat Related Resources - 211info
text: Summer and Heat Related Resources - 211info This page contains summer and heat-related resources based on the information shared with 211info by providers as resources become available. Check this page frequently for the most up-to-date information. Opening hours are based on specific counties’ and individual agencies’ criteria. Resources listed on this page can and will change depending on the current event and availability of resources. In addition to cooling centers, local community centers, libraries, and churches may be open to the public during times of extreme heat. Splash pads and shopping centers can also be a place to cool off during the day. If you know of a resource that is not listed, or information that needs to be updated, please contact our Resource Team at 503-416-2698 or by email: [email protected] . title: Volunteering & Donating - 211info
text: Volunteering & Donating - 211info Give to 211info Donate to support the 211info team in its day-to-day work . When you give help, you give hope. Make a difference in your community by donating your time and gently-used items to Oregonians in need or make a tax-deductible gift to 211info. Donate your time. From delivering meals to mentoring kids to nature restoration, there are so many ways to use your time and talents for others. Use the Volunteering category drop down below to find volunteer opportunities in your community. Donate the right gear. Help keep your neighbors fed, dry, and safe. Outreach providers need gear they can pass out now and to have in reserve during cold weather. Having the right gear staged in the right places before a crisis will help providers better focus on direct services and life-saving efforts in the moment. Use the Donating category drop down below to find donation opportunities in your community. < New Search Volunteering Disaster Related Volunteer Preparedness/Mobilization Programs X Disaster Related Volunteer Preparedness/Mobilization Programs Programs, primarily national in scope, that work to recruit, prepare, mobilize and/or coordinate the activ

title: Financial Wellness - 211info
text: Financial Wellness - 211info Protect your money and build a better future. Every Oregonian deserves an opportunity to get ahead and to be more financially secure. Through a partnership with Oregon State Treasury and the Oregon Jump$tart Coalition, 211info is providing easier access to organizations and government programs that can help you make more confident decisions and strengthen your financial wellbeing. If your organization offers financial services, please list it with 211info . < New Search Financial coaching & education Money Management & Financial Literacy X Would you like to add a location? title: News Archives - 211info
text: News Archives - 211info Food insecurity is a growing concern for many as prices continue to climb. But for older adults on fixed incomes, even basics like fresh, healthy food can feel out of reach. That’s where the Senior Farm Direct Nutrition Program (SFDNP) can help. SFDNP provides eligible seniors with vouchers to buy locally grown fruits, vegetables, and... Read More Whether you are financially planning ahead, catching up after the holidays, or trying to make sense of tax season, free and low-cost resources are available to help with both financial literacy and tax preparation. This includes help understanding tax credits, finding reliable filing options, and building knowledge that supports long-term stability and financial resilience. Knowing... Read More For our Community Outreach and Engagement Team, the core of the work is meeting communities where they are, listening first, and learning how to make it easier for people to understand how 211info exists to remove the guesswork. Through relationship-building, local presence, and targeted engagement, our team works to ensure that 211info is understood as... Read More As of January 1, 2026, 211info serves all 36 counties in Or

title: Air Conditioners - 211info
text: Air Conditioners - 211info - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search 22 Matching Service Providers The mapped locations are estimates, for the exact location of an individual site, select "More Details" Accessibility Options - Select - No Filter Wheelchair Access (Full) Wheelchair Access (Partial) Accessible Parking (Private Lot) Accessible Parking (Nearby Public/Street Parking) Accessible Restrooms Alternative Communication Formats Virtual Service Options Staff Trained in Disability Awareness ADVANCED HEALTH HEALTH RELATED SOCIAL NEEDS CLIMATE SUPPORTS Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Climate Support is a health-related social need (HRSN) service and is provided by the Oregon Health Plan. HRSN Climate Supports are devices to help keep people safe during climate events. Covered devices are only for use in the member's home or primary residence and may include: -Air conditioners, heaters, and air filters to provide clean air and healthy temperatures -Mini fridges to keep medication cold during a power outage -Portable power supplies to operate medical devices like ventilators during a power outage Members can only receive HRS

title: Multnomah County Summer & Heat Related Resources - 211info
text: Multnomah County Summer & Heat Related Resources - 211info UPDATED: May 1, 2026, at 6:11 PM Cooling centers are not open at this time. Multnomah County and the City of Portland will continue to monitor weather conditions via the National Weather Service and send future alerts as necessary. Year-round resources remain available. Help for When It’s Hot – Multnomah County Cooling Resources When Does Multnomah County Open Cooling Centers or Shelters? Cool Spaces Near You Interactive map of cool spaces , including: library branches, pools, community centers, splash pads, misting stations, and fountains. Sign Up for Alerts Sign up for text alerts from 211info when weather alerts are called in Multnomah County, TEXT the keyword “pdxshelter” to 898211 (TXT211) Sign up to receive email alerts from 211info when weather alerts are called in Multnomah County Year-Round Shelters & Daytime Drop-In Centers Locate year-round shelters and daytime drop-in centers
